items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` **Triggered by:** PR #57627 by `@radhgupta` ## What Changed PR #57627 migrated the `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` package to the new TypeSpec emitter, regenerating client code, updating the README, and adding/updating 23 sample `.md` files. The README was substantially updated but several gaps remain. ## Gaps Found - **Template placeholder not removed**: The `## Getting started` section still contains the boilerplate text `"This section should include everything a developer needs to do to install and create their first client connection *very quickly*."` — this must be removed. - **Broken NuGet package link**: `[package]: (www.nuget.org/redacted) is missing the package name; should be `(www.nuget.org/redacted) - **Broken API reference link**: `[text_refdocs]: (learn.microsoft.com/redacted) is incomplete and does not resolve to the actual API docs. - **No inline code snippet in the Examples section**: The `## Examples` section only lists links to external `.md` sample files. The canonical README pattern includes at least one inline `C# Snippet:` example (backed by a test file) so users see a quick-start code block directly in the README without navigating away.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/README.md` — edit **Change 1 — Remove template placeholder** Find and remove this line under `## Getting started`: ```text This section should include everything a developer needs to do to install and create their first client connection *very quickly*. ``` **Change 2 — Fix NuGet package link** (bottom of file, `` section) Replace: ```text [package]: (www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` With: ```text [package]: (www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` **Change 3 — Fix API reference link** (bottom of file, `` section) Replace: ```text [text_refdocs]: (learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` With: ```text [text_refdocs]: (learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` **Change 4 — Add inline snippet placeholder in `## Examples`** After the list of sample links in `## Examples`, add a short "Create a project" quick-start snippet placeholder: ```markdown ### Create a project ```C# Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject ``` ``` (closing triple-backtick on its own line) ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The test file `sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/tests/Samples/Sample1_ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject.cs` likely already contains a sample method. Wrap the relevant portion in a region so the snippet generator can extract it: ```csharp #region Snippet:ConversationsAuthoring_CreateProject // ... the core create-project code (no test setup, assertions, or cleanup) ... #endregion ``` Any test-only code (setup, teardown, assertions) must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` so it is excluded from the rendered README. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical example of the `#region Snippet:` / `#if !SNIPPET` pattern. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples — **needs inline snippet added** (see above) 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed beyond the four edits above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitivelanguage` — confirm the `## Examples` snippet block in README.md is populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitivelanguage` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/`
## Service Contacts `@ahmedaashour` `@bidisha-c` `@mikaelsitruk` `@quentinRobinson` `@sarkar-rajarshi` ### Suggested next steps Consider assigning this issue to **Copilot coding agent** for implementation.
items.title: [Conversations.Authoring] Docs: Fix broken links and missing inline examples in README
